subject: Sociology
title: Feminist Perspectives on Education
description: A short lesson to help you apply Feminist Perspectives on Education.

hook.text: Girls outperform boys in most school systems now. But peek at physics class and the old pattern appears.

buildup.text: Feminists have studied how schools reproduce gender roles through textbooks, expectations, and subject choices.

discovery.text: Gender stereotyping channels girls away from STEM and boys away from arts, limiting both groups' potential.

twist.text: Girls' academic success hasn't eliminated the pay gap. Education alone doesn't dismantle workplace inequality.

climax.text: Schools can challenge or reinforce gender norms. The outcome depends on curricula, teachers, and culture.

punchline.text: Grades rose; the glass ceiling stayed.

quiz.question: What do feminists critique about education?
options: How schools reproduce gender stereotypes and limit choices
